qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum number of cores supported for a single Ansys Additive Manufacturing job on Gra-vdi?
qa_pairs.answer: A single Ansys Additive Manufacturing job on Gra-vdi can utilize up to 16 cores.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to run an Ansys Additive Manufacturing job on Gra-vdi?
qa_pairs.answer: To run an Ansys Additive Manufacturing job on Gra-vdi, first start Workbench as described in the 'Enable Additive' section. Then, open your project file (e.g., `test.wbpj`), reset the workspace if the screen is grey (View -> reset workspace), start Mechanical, clear generated data, tick 'Distributed', specify the desired number of cores, and finally, click 'File -> Save Project -> Solve'.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check the resource utilization of an Ansys Additive Manufacturing job on Gra-vdi?
qa_pairs.answer: To check resource utilization, open another terminal window and run either `top -u $USER` or `ps u -u $USER | grep ansys`.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I get a grey screen after opening my project file in Workbench on Gra-vdi?
qa_pairs.answer: If you get a grey screen after opening your project file in Workbench on Gra-vdi, click 'View -> reset workspace'.
